title: Two Sum
prompt: Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return the indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`. Assume exactly one solution exists, and you may not use the same element twice.

title: Valid Parentheses
prompt: Given a string containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid (every open bracket is closed by the same type, in the correct order).

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
prompt: Merge two sorted linked lists and return the merged list as one sorted linked list, built by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.

title: Binary Search
prompt: Given a sorted array of integers `nums` and a target value, return the index of target if it exists, otherwise return -1. Solve it in O(log n) time.

title: Maximum Subarray
prompt: Given an integer array `nums`, find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) which has the largest sum, and return that sum.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
prompt: Given a string `s`, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

title: 3Sum
prompt: Given an integer array `nums`, return all the triplets `[nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]]` such that i != j != k and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0. The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.

title: Linked List Cycle
prompt: Given the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it, without using extra space (Floyd's cycle detection).

title: Top K Frequent Elements
prompt: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k` most frequent elements. Try to beat O(n log n) time complexity.

title: Coin Change
prompt: Given an array of coin denominations and a target amount, return the fewest number of coins needed to make up that amount. Return -1 if it cannot be made.

title: Number of Islands
prompt: Given an `m x n` 2D binary grid representing a map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands (connected land, 4-directionally).

title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
prompt: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the kth largest element in the array (kth largest in sorted order, not the kth distinct element).
